# Security — was this built securely?

**Verdict:** ship-with-caution — The security architecture is strong and the core trust boundaries hold in code; no reachable high/critical issue exists under the single-user on-device threat model. Ship after the two cheap fixes (keychain error redaction + cross-origin token guard) and after explicitly tracking the known-deferred conformance-in-prose gap.

> **✅ Remediation (2026-06-15):** all actionable items are fixed + tested. Keychain
> write-error redaction (`keychain.ts`); same-origin token-exfiltration backstop +
> graceful cross-origin pagination stop (`canvas/http.ts`); esbuild→0.28.1 (0 vulns,
> `audit:prod` gate); CSP `base-uri 'none'; form-action 'none'`; global permission-deny
> + `web-contents-created` navigation guard (`main.ts`); `view.text` conformance claims
> now LABELLED as non-authoritative (only the gate badge certifies); `describe_image`
> decoded-size guard. See [SHIP-READINESS.md](./SHIP-READINESS.md#-remediation-status-2026-06-15--all-8-conditions-addressed).

## Summary

This pillar is in good shape. The load-bearing invariants are all verified in code: the output gate is unconditional, server-side, and fail-closed (C2 holds); all SQL is parameterized with no injectable surface; the Canvas token lives only in main, never reaches the renderer or disk in plaintext on the success paths, and is handled via an arg-array `security` CLI call with no shell (C7 holds); uploads-dir confinement (C6) is airtight; the Electron renderer is hardened (contextIsolation + nodeIntegration:false + strict CSP + script-disabled sandboxed preview iframe); and the IPC surface is a fixed 14-method namespaced allowlist. The confirmed problems are all **low/info**: four genuine but bounded defense-in-depth gaps (a keychain token leak on a write-failure error path, a cross-origin token-follow on Link pagination, the ungated conformance-prose over-claim, and a dev-only esbuild HIGH that never ships) plus a set of positive "healthy" confirmations. Two of the four actionable fixes are cheap and worth doing before ship; one (conformance-prose) is a documented, deferred product-decision item relevant to the app's compliance value proposition.

## Confirmed findings

Ordered by threat-model-adjusted severity. The first five are actionable gaps; the remainder are verified positive controls recorded so the reader can trust coverage.

### Actionable (low / info)

| Severity | Title | Location | Recommendation |
|---|---|---|---|
| low | Token leaks into execFile error message and crosses IPC to the renderer on a Keychain **write** failure | `src/storage/keychain.ts:80-92` (set has no try/catch); `src/app/ipc.ts:75-87`; `src/app/bridge.ts:49-55` | Wrap `set()`'s `run()` in try/catch and rethrow a redacted error (drop message/cmd/stack), or pass the token via stdin. Cheap; closes a real invariant-#4 violation. |
| low | Prompt-injected WCAG conformance claim reaches the UI via **ungated `view.text`** | `src/runtime/app-api.ts:377-383` (`text: turn.text` ungated; only fragments hit `enforceGate`), persisted at `341-346` | Freeze a TurnView contract with a typed gate-owned conformance field, or scrub/label conformance phrases in prose. Text-only — **not** XSS; badge stays correctly withheld. Known-deferred. |
| low | Bearer token attached to a **cross-origin** `Link: rel=next` follow with no host check | `src/canvas/http.ts:50-55, 69-81`; `src/canvas/importer.ts:108-132`; `src/canvas/pages.ts:76-94` | Assert `new URL(next).origin === new URL(base).origin` before following and before attaching the token; stop pagination otherwise. Cheap. |
| low | Dev-only HIGH advisory in esbuild (GHSA-gv7w-rqvm-qjhr) — never ships but trips the audit gate | `package.json:20-30`; `package-lock.json:2213-2217, :4054`; `node_modules/esbuild@0.28.0` | `npm audit fix` -> esbuild 0.28.1 (non-breaking under tsx's `~0.28.0`). Not in the DMG; prod deps are only axe-core + playwright. |
| info | Token passed as an argv value to `security add-generic-password` (briefly visible in the process table, write path only) | `src/storage/keychain.ts:80-92` | Move to `spawn` + stdin (`-w` last, no value) — same fix as the error-leak above. Marginal risk: a same-user process can already read the item prompt-free. |
| info | CSP omits `base-uri` and `form-action` (neither inherits from `default-src`) | `src/app/renderer/index.html:13-16` | Add `base-uri 'none'; form-action 'none';`. Redundant backstop — allowlist already strips `<base>`/`<form>` and will-navigate blocks off-app submits. |

### Verified positive controls (info — recorded for coverage)

| Area | What was confirmed | Location |
|---|---|---|
| Output gate | Unconditional, server-side, fail-closed; both `blocker` and `error` (axe critical+serious) withhold the badge (C2); a throwing auditor rejects `runTurn` rather than silently passing | `src/orchestrator/gate.ts:68-92`; `src/runtime/app-api.ts:368-375, 411-429`; `src/app/unavailable-api.ts:23-71` |
| innerHTML sink | Sole `innerHTML` write (`gatedBody`) receives only gate-approved HTML; prose is `textContent`; CSP `script-src 'self'` is the backstop | `src/app/renderer/conversation.ts:61-66`; `preview.ts:30-36`; `index.html:13-16` |
| SQL | All values bound; the only DDL identifier interpolation uses compile-time constant tuples; FTS5 scope clause interpolates only `?` placeholders + a constant BM25 expression | `src/storage/database.ts:35-46`; `schema.ts:85-138`; `src/knowledge/retriever.ts:138-173` |
| Token isolation | Token held only in main, never returned across the read IPC surface, never written to SQLite/disk in plaintext (success paths) | `src/runtime/app-api.ts:272-285, 466-495`; `src/app/bridge.ts:116-122`; `src/storage/keychain.ts` |
| Keychain CLI | Arg-array `execFile`, no shell; not argument-injectable; account key always `canvas-token:`-prefixed (C7 holds) | `src/storage/keychain.ts:34-40, 57-104`; `app-api.ts:199-201` |
| Uploads confinement | Empty/absolute/`..`/leading-space/`~`/`%2e` all rejected or contained; single live caller via `ingestDocument`; `convertUrl` SSRF latent (no caller) | `src/ingest/safe-path.ts:28-42`; `src/runtime/deps.ts:120` |
| Electron renderer | contextIsolation:true + nodeIntegration:false + typed bridge + strict CSP; `sandbox:false` residual bounded (ESM-preload constraint); will-navigate + setWindowOpenHandler deny external | `src/app/main.ts:28-63` |
| IPC surface | Fixed 14-method namespaced allowlist; no wildcard/dynamic handlers; raw `ipcRenderer` never exposed | `src/app/ipc.ts:93-157`; `channels.ts:11-37`; `preload.ts:17-27`; `bridge.ts:58-124` |
| Supply chain | Lockfile fully pinned (resolved + SHA-512 SRI on all deps), single registry, no overrides, no `.npmrc`; install/postinstall scripts all mainstream and dev/build-only | `package-lock.json` (lockfileVersion 3); `package.json` |
| Licensing | axe-core MPL-2.0 used unmodified (file-level copyleft satisfied), LICENSE preserved, no vendored fork | `src/engine/render/playwright-runner.ts:150-151`; `node_modules/axe-core/LICENSE` |
| ReDoS | No catastrophic-backtracking shapes in allowlist/contrast/http regexes; worst case is benign quadratic on whitespace runs | `src/engine/allowlist.ts`; `contrast.ts`; `app-api.ts:171`; `canvas/http.ts:75-77` |
| Latent items | RCDATA/RAWTEXT/PLAINTEXT modeling gap is a fidelity divergence (over-parses, never under-parses — cannot become an escape); convertUrl SSRF is latent dead code; auditor image-contrast fail-open is disclosed (warning/needs-review), not silent | `src/engine/allowlist.ts:122-126, 460-492`; `src/ingest/client.ts:38-40`; `src/engine/render/auditor.ts:33-34` |

## Checked & cleared / refuted

These were investigated and found NOT to be real security defects, so the reader knows the gaps were probed:

- **esbuild HIGH RCE — not a shipped exposure.** Real CVE, but the vulnerable Deno-install path + Windows dev-server vectors are unreachable on macOS; esbuild is dev-only (via tsx/electron-builder) and excluded from the DMG (`build.files` = `dist/**/*`, asar:true). Kept as a low hygiene item, not a product risk.
- **Playwright Chromium "pinning gap" — REFUTED.** The browser revision IS pinned via `playwright-core/browsers.json` shipped inside the lockfile-locked `playwright` package; the download is a dev/build-time step kept behind an env-gated test, absent from the shipped runtime, no remote-attacker path. The only residual is the **functional** packaging to-do (extraResources must bundle Chromium or the audit gate ships non-functional) — already a known-open item, not a supply-chain vulnerability.
- **Electron 42.3.3 patch cadence — REFUTED as a finding.** Facts (pinned `^42.3.3`, bundles Chromium, ships as the shell) are accurate, but the load-bearing claim — that 42.3.3 lags a supported patch line — is self-marked unverified with no CVE. It is a pre-release version-cadence recommendation, not a confirmed defect.
- **DDL string interpolation in `schema.ts` — non-issue.** The interpolated `ALTER TABLE ${table} ADD COLUMN ${column} ${type}` draws only from hard-coded constant tuples; all values are bound. No SQL injection surface anywhere, including the FTS5 retriever.
- **convertUrl SSRF — latent, no live caller.** `convertUrl`/`http_sources` exist but no orchestrator tool or app-api method invokes them; a prompt-injected model cannot reach them. Only `convertPath` (uploads-confined local file) is wired. Would become real only if a URL-ingest tool is ever added.
- **Ungated `view.text` is not an XSS/sandbox-escape vector.** Prose renders via `textContent`/text-node append (never `innerHTML`); CSP `script-src 'self'` is only a secondary defense. It is purely a semantic trust/honesty gap, correctly scoped (see low finding above).

## Action items before ship

Prioritized. Each fix has a paired regression test so the behavior is locked.

**P0 — cheap, do before ship**

1. **Redact the Keychain write-failure error.** Fix: wrap `set()`'s `run()` in try/catch in `src/storage/keychain.ts:80-92` and rethrow a redacted error (discard `message`/`cmd`/`stack`); ideally switch to `spawn` + stdin so the token also leaves argv.
   - Test: `keychain leak redaction` -> `test/storage/keychain.test.ts` -> inject a `CommandRunner` that rejects with an Error whose `message`/`cmd` contain `THE_TOKEN`; call `saveCanvasAuth({token:'THE_TOKEN'})` through the IPC envelope and assert the surfaced error name+message do NOT contain `THE_TOKEN`. Effort: cheap. Blocker: yes (real invariant-#4 violation).

2. **Origin-check the Canvas `Link: rel=next` follow.** Fix: in `src/canvas/http.ts` / `parseLinkNext` consumers, assert `new URL(next).origin === new URL(base).origin` before following and before attaching `Authorization`.
   - Test: `cross-origin pagination drop` -> `test/canvas/http.test.ts` -> fake fetch returns `Link: <https://evil.test/next>; rel="next"`; assert no second request goes to `evil.test` (or it carries no `Authorization`). Effort: cheap. Blocker: no, but do it (token-exfil defense-in-depth).

3. **Bump esbuild to clear the audit gate.** Fix: `npm audit fix` (esbuild 0.28.0 -> 0.28.1). Add CI step `npm audit --omit=dev --audit-level=high` asserting exit 0 so a future HIGH in a *production* dep fails the build while dev-only noise is excluded.
   - Effort: trivial. Blocker: no.

**P1 — track explicitly; product/data-model decision**

4. **Gate or label conformance claims in `view.text`.** The documented frozen-TurnView fix (typed gate-owned conformance field, or prose scrubber) addresses the only finding that touches the compliance-trust value prop given ADA Title II deadlines. Until then, ship a persistent "prose is unverified" UI disclaimer.
   - Test: `prose conformance not asserted` -> `test/runtime/app-api.test.ts` -> scripted chatRunner whose `turn.text` = "This page is WCAG 2.2 AA certified." with no ```html block; assert the returned `TurnView.text` is scrubbed/labelled and no badge is implied. Effort: medium. Blocker: no (documented deferred item), but track.
   - Lock the gate's fail-closed behavior: `throwing audit rejects turn` -> assert `createAppApi` with a rejecting `gate.audit` makes `runTurn` reject and emit zero fragments/badges. Effort: cheap.

**P2 — free hardening / when packaging is executed**

5. Add `base-uri 'none'; form-action 'none';` to the renderer CSP (`index.html:14`) — redundant but free.
6. Add `session.setPermissionRequestHandler(cb=>cb(false))` and an `app.on('web-contents-created', ...)` guard so any future window/webview inherits the policy (no live gap today — webviewTag off, setWindowOpenHandler denies all).
7. When macOS packaging is first executed: ensure `extraResources` bundles the pinned-revision Chromium (or the build fails) so the audit gate ships functional; verify the DMG includes `node_modules/axe-core/LICENSE` and add an attribution/About entry (axe-core MPL-2.0, playwright Apache-2.0, electron MIT).
